name: Artha Rini

notes: The current restaurant-issued boundary is 75 main-menu products, six Liwetan offerings, five Gudeg offerings, three configurable Rijsttafel packages, twelve Food Stall offerings, seventeen Rice Boxes, two configurable Tumpeng packages, and forty Jajanan Pasar snacks.

All 62 frozen rows were reconciled: 21 exact matches, 29 normalized malformed labels, and twelve non-product artifacts. They cover 49 unique current products; 111 current offerings were absent from the frozen app catalog.

The twelve artifacts were section headings, ordering instructions, a restaurant masthead, price/modifier fragments, or duplicate description fragments. Package components remain inside their configurable package instead of becoming standalone products.
Food-stall and main-menu items with the same name remain distinct service-specific offerings where their published composition or purchase format differs.
Beverages and desserts remain last in main-menu category order.

All 62 frozen rows were reconciled against all 160 current owner-published offerings: 21 exact matches, 29 normalized malformed labels, twelve artifacts, 49 unique current products represented, and 111 omissions restored.
Eight source surfaces were retained with exact boundaries: main 75, Liwetan 6, Gudeg 5, Rijsttafel 3, Food Stall 12, Rice Box 17, Tumpeng 2, and Jajanan Pasar 40.

Artha Rini's current main menu repeats an establishment-wide warning that food may contain or contact peanuts, tree nuts, wheat, dairy, egg, soy, fish, shellfish, and sesame. It is represented as cross-contact on all 160 current offerings, not as fixed ingredients or absence evidence.
The corrected catalog has 113 products with direct restaurant-menu ingredient or explicit formulation signals and 47 products supported only by the global cross-contact disclosure.

Every one of the fifty non-artifact frozen rows omitted the current global cross-contact warning. Frozen fixed signals were also smeared across adjacent rows, including wheat/gluten/egg on satays and shellfish/peanut on Pempek Kapal Selam.

Gluten-free and vegan labels do not override the global cross-contact warning. Coconut and melinjo seed are not promoted to tree-nut claims; optional almond milk is represented as tree nut only where the menu expressly offers it.
Menu silence is never treated as a negative allergen claim.
All 160 products carry the restaurant's explicit establishment-wide cross-contact warning; 113 also have direct positive ingredient/formulation signals and 47 remain global-cross-contact-only.
The frozen catalog omitted cross-contact on every non-artifact row and contained adjacent-row fixed-allergen smear. No menu silence, vegan label, or gluten-free label is promoted to absence evidence.

The frozen PDF extraction lost product names and promoted nearby description lines, section headings, price prefixes, modifiers, masthead text, and ordering instructions into products. Forty-three of 62 rows were then mislabeled as Salads.

The frozen extraction used an obsolete July 2025 main PDF and omitted the current 2026 main menu, the forty-item Jajanan Pasar catalog, most special/catering surfaces, and the establishment-wide warning.
The reproducible audit builder verifies the hash and exact product boundary of all eight owner PDFs and fails closed unless the catalog contains exactly 160 unique products.
Four focused catalog, reconciliation, and repaired-output tests pass. Consecutive target-only repair runs produce the same target hash and leave the non-target restaurant projection unchanged.
The hash-pinned builder fails closed unless all eight restaurant-issued PDFs retain their reviewed hashes and the canonical catalog remains exactly 160 unique products.
Four focused tests pass, the target-only repair is idempotent, and the non-target restaurant projection is unchanged.

sourceAttempts.outcome: The owner menu index currently links eight restaurant-issued PDFs: 2026 main, 2025 Liwetan, 2025 Gudeg, 2024 Rijsttafel, 2024 Food Stall, Rice Box, Tumpeng, and 2026 Jajanan Pasar.
All 23 pages were captured, rendered, and visually reviewed. The source contains 75 products and repeats the establishment-wide allergen warning on every food page.

Liwetan, Gudeg, Rijsttafel, Food Stall, Rice Box, and Tumpeng PDFs were captured, hashed, text-extracted, rendered, and visually reviewed, yielding 45 orderable offerings after preserving package boundaries.
All five pages were captured, rendered, and visually reviewed. Forty snacks are orderable; minimum-order, shipping, equipment, and reconfirmation notes are instructions.

findings.summary: The frozen catalog contained twelve document artifacts and represented only 49 of 160 current products.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt the complete eight-surface catalog, removed all twelve fake rows, restored 111 omissions, and preserved components inside configurable packages.

findings.summary: Every non-artifact frozen row omitted the restaurant's current establishment-wide cross-contact warning.
findings.resolution: Applied the exact published warning as mayContain on all 160 offerings, mapping dairy to the app's milk identifier and pairing wheat with gluten without inventing other allergens.

findings.summary: The frozen parser smeared nearby terms across items, producing unsupported fixed allergens and missing direct ingredient signals.
findings.resolution: Re-reviewed all current item text, retained 113 direct positive ingredient/formulation rows, and left 47 rows as global-cross-contact-only instead of inferring fixed ingredients.

findings.summary: The frozen app relied on an obsolete July 2025 main PDF and incomplete special/catering sources while the owner now publishes a 2026 main menu and 2026 Jajanan catalog.
findings.resolution: Pinned the current menu index and every linked source by SHA-256 and made the repair fail closed on any hash or product-boundary change.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt all 160 current offerings, corrected fixed allergen semantics and global cross-contact provenance, removed twelve artifacts, and left every non-target restaurant unchanged.
